content.problemStatement:
Now we will implement a reporting system where users can flag inappropriate comments. The system will automatically hide a comment after it receives a certain number of unique reports.

**After this task:** 
- A **"Report"** button will appear on comments made by other users.
- When a user clicks **"Report"**, their report is recorded in the database. 
- A user can only report a specific comment once.
- If a comment accumulates 5 unique reports, its `is_hidden_by_spam` flag will be set to True.
- Hidden comments will display a message like "[This comment is hidden due to spam reports.]" instead of their original content.
- The "Report" button will not be shown on a user's own comments or on comments that are already hidden.
### Files to Create/Modify for this Task:
- comment_system/settings.py (**Done for you)** *(Line #128 & #129)*
   - Add the report threshold to your custom settings section at the end of the file.
- comments/models.py 
   - Modify the Comment model to include a boolean flag for hiding and a ManyToManyField to track which users have reported it.
- comments/views.py 
   - Add the new `report_comment` view. 
   - This view contains the logic to prevent self-reporting, add a user to the reports list, and check if the threshold has been met.
- comments/urls.py 
   - Add the URL pattern for the new report action.
- comments/templates/comments/comment_display.html **(Done for you)** 
   - Updated the template to:
     - Add the hidden class if a comment is hidden by spam.
     - Display the hidden message.
     - Conditionally show the "Report" button.

**After completing the above:**
- Click on **RUN** and open the browser on a new tab.
- Create new users via the signup page. (This is best tested with multiple user accounts.)
- Log in as User A and post a comment.
- Log out and log in as User B. Go to the same page, find User A's comment, and click **"Report"**.
- Repeat this process for Users C, D, and E.
- When the fifth user (User E) reports the comment, the page should reload, and the comment's content should be replaced with the hidden message.
